authoritative_sources.title: Publication 15 (2026), Circular E, Employer's Tax Guide
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IRS Publication 15 is the primary federal employer payroll tax guide for withholding, deposit schedules, employment-tax returns, payroll service provider boundaries, recordkeeping, and penalty-sensitive payroll tax workflow.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: payroll tax setup, deposit readiness, Form 941 support, completion evidence, and tax exception handling
used_for: employment tax filing and liability checks
payroll service provider and employer responsibility boundaries
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains payroll-run readiness, tax remittance risk, and completion scenarios so the lane cannot skip deposit, filing, liability, or employer-responsibility controls.

authoritative_sources.title: Publication 15-T (2026), Federal Income Tax Withholding Methods
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IRS Publication 15-T is the official source for federal withholding calculation methods using pay frequency, taxable wages, and Form W-4 inputs.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: withholding setup validation and gross-to-net processing support
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Forces the lane to require withholding forms and payroll-period facts before treating gross-to-net calculations as supportable.

authoritative_sources.title: Instructions for Form 941, Employer's Quarterly Federal Tax Return
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IRS Form 941 instructions are authoritative for employment-tax filing package content, quarterly liability reporting, deposit reconciliation, and correction-sensitive return support.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: quarterly filing support, payroll register tie-out, and completion evidence capture
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Shapes completion evidence scenarios by requiring payroll register and deposit support without letting the lane sign or certify the return.

authoritative_sources.title: Instructions for Forms W-2 and W-3
authoritative_sources.publisher: Social Security Administration and Internal Revenue Service
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official W-2/W-3 instructions govern wage and tax statement content, transmittal handling, corrections, and year-end payroll reporting artifacts.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: year-end payroll reporting support and wage statement evidence review
used_for: wage box and tax statement terminology
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Keeps payroll reporting scenarios tied to wage statement evidence rather than generic annual reporting language.

authoritative_sources.title: 29 CFR Part 516, Records to Be Kept by Employers
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 29 CFR Part 516 is the federal wage-hour recordkeeping regulation for payroll records, hours worked, pay basis, additions, deductions, wages paid, and pay periods.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: input evidence validation, workpaper support review, and record-retention controls
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains missing-input scenarios so payroll services cannot rely on unsupported hours, rates, additions, deductions, or pay-period evidence.

authoritative_sources.title: Fact Sheet #21: Recordkeeping Requirements under the Fair Labor Standards Act
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Labor, Wage and Hour Division
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DOL Wage and Hour Division guidance gives public operating detail for payroll records, hours, wage bases, additions, deductions, payment dates, and retention that payroll services must verify.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Supports the exception queue scenario for conflicting timekeeping, HRIS, and pay-rate records.

authoritative_sources.title: Statements on Standards for Tax Services
authoritative_sources.publisher: American Institute of Certified Public Accountants
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AICPA SSTS are recognized professional tax-service standards for CPA tax work and are authoritative for diligence, reliance on information, error handling, communication, and advisory boundaries in payroll tax service contexts.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: professional diligence, client communication, and tax-service boundary screening
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains advisory-boundary scenarios so payroll services can package evidence but cannot substitute operational support for professional tax conclusions.

authoritative_sources.title: Treasury Department Circular No. 230
authoritative_sources.publisher: Internal Revenue Service, Office of Professional Responsibility
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Circular 230 governs practice before the IRS and is authoritative for practitioner diligence, representation authority, and boundaries when payroll service work intersects IRS-facing tax matters.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: authority screening, quality review, and escalation for tax-practitioner judgment
used_for: review discipline for tax-sensitive payroll outputs
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents the lane from signing returns, advising on penalty strategy, or representing clients before the IRS from an execution queue.

authoritative_sources.title: Publication 4557, Safeguarding Taxpayer Data
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IRS Publication 4557 is official taxpayer-data safeguarding guidance for tax professionals and is authoritative for secure handling, access control, incident awareness, and confidentiality of payroll tax records.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: confidentiality screening, secure delivery, and client data handling
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Shapes confidentiality scenarios by requiring approved secure channels and escalation for unauthorized disclosure requests.

workflow_stages.name: Payroll intake and prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Classify the request, confirm client engagement scope, entity, jurisdiction, pay period, pay frequency, payroll calendar, deposit schedule, filing model, approval matrix, and systems of record before accepting the workflow.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
Professional standards or recognized public frameworks

workflow_stages.name: Input evidence and source-of-truth review
workflow_stages.description: Validate timesheets, HRIS status, pay rates, withholding forms, deductions, direct-deposit setup, payroll calendar, and approval records against payroll and timekeeping systems before gross-to-net processing.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
Professional standards or recognized public frameworks

workflow_stages.name: Payroll processing and register tie-out
workflow_stages.description: Compare gross-to-net outputs, taxes, deductions, payroll register variances, employee setup, and pay-period records to determine whether the run can advance, hold, or rework.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
Professional standards or recognized public frameworks

workflow_stages.name: Tax deposit, filing, and payment readiness
workflow_stages.description: Prepare deposit and filing readiness checks using employment-tax liabilities, deposit schedule, EFTPS or payment evidence, Form 941 support, W-2/W-3 support, and reviewer or payment approval controls without signing or authorizing funds.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Exception queue handling and authority escalation
workflow_stages.description: Hold or route incomplete inputs, unsupported pay changes, tax timing risk, confidentiality conflicts, source conflicts, and advisory or signoff requests to the correct owner instead of improvising authority.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks

workflow_stages.name: Completion confirmation and evidence capture
workflow_stages.description: Produce a completion evidence record only after the final register, payment or funding status, deposit or filing evidence, reviewer signoff, exception disposition, and retention-ready workpaper references are captured.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

artifacts: client engagement scope, payroll calendar, approval matrix, jurisdiction list, entity identifiers, and deposit schedule
timesheets, time clock exports, PTO records, schedules, overtime support, and exception approvals
HRIS employee master data, employment status, pay-rate records, earnings codes, deduction codes, garnishment or benefit deduction authorizations, and direct-deposit setup
Forms W-4, state withholding forms, employee tax setup records, and withholding method support
gross-to-net outputs, payroll registers, variance reports, prior payroll history, off-cycle correction records, and exception logs
employment-tax deposit schedules, EFTPS or payment confirmations, Form 941 or 944 support, W-2 and W-3 support, and agency filing errors
reviewer signoffs, quality review notes, confidentiality constraints, secure-delivery confirmations, and retained payroll workpaper references
systems_of_record: tax filing and agency correspondence system
EFTPS, banking, ACH, or payment approval system
document management and payroll workpaper repository
practice management or client engagement workflow system
approval registry and exception queue tracker
secure client communication or portal system

allowed: Classify payroll service requests as in scope, out of scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Request the minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, approval evidence, systems of record, and source context needed before payroll action.
Validate input completeness and consistency across timesheets, HRIS, withholding forms, deduction authorizations, payroll registers, tax filing support, and payment evidence.
Advance, hold, rework, or complete payroll workflow states based on documented execution state and evidence.
Prepare payroll-run readiness summaries, completion summaries, exception queue notes, tax exception notes, and escalation packets without overstating authority.
escalate: Payroll inputs are incomplete, stale, unapproved, or conflict with approved records.
A pay change, correction, off-cycle payment, deduction, reversal, void, or direct-deposit action lacks required authorization.
Tax deposit, remittance, filing, or payment timing creates unresolved compliance risk.
Federal, state, local, tenant, client engagement, or professional rules materially conflict.

The request requires tax advice, legal interpretation, worker classification, return signature, payment approval, contractual signoff, HR policy decision, attestation, or professional judgment outside the lane.
Confidentiality rules, approved channels, client data commitments, or cross-tenant boundaries block safe delivery.
refuse: Process payroll, apply a pay change, alter a withholding setup, approve a correction, or mark a run complete without required records and approvals.
Authorize funds movement, direct deposits, reversals, voids, tax payments, or failed-payment overrides without explicit payment authority.
Sign employment tax returns, provide tax or legal advice, decide worker classification, negotiate penalty relief, or certify audit or attest conclusions.
Disclose payroll, employee, taxpayer, or client data outside approved tenant channels or without proper audience authority.
Resolve material source or policy conflicts by guessing.

domain_failure_modes: processing employees from incomplete or conflicting timesheet, HRIS, pay-rate, or approval records
applying a pay change, bonus, deduction, withholding change, or off-cycle correction without documented authorization
missing or late employment-tax deposits because deposit schedule, liability date, cutoff, or payment evidence was not checked
marking payroll complete without final register, payment, deposit, filing, reviewer, or workpaper evidence
disclosing payroll registers, SSNs, wage data, or taxpayer information through unapproved channels or to an unauthorized audience
drifting from operational payroll service support into tax advice, legal advice, worker-classification judgment, return signature, payment approval, or attestation authority
flattening federal, state, local, tenant, and engagement-specific payroll rules into a generic payroll process when they materially differ

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Client request that crosses into advisory or signoff authority
Confidentiality or approval conflict in delivery workflow
Payroll inputs are incomplete or conflict with approved records.
A pay change or correction lacks required authorization.
Tax or remittance timing creates unresolved compliance risk.
Normal in-scope execution from accepted work to completion.
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State, local, industry-specific wage-hour, withholding, garnishment, final-pay, and paid-leave rules
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Rules vary materially by jurisdiction and client workforce. The pack requires tenant-supplied jurisdiction facts and authoritative state or local sources before applying these rules to a payroll run.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant payroll platform configuration and approval authority
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Payroll system roles, funding authority, direct-deposit reversal authority, approval matrix, and reviewer queues are tenant-specific. The lane cannot infer them from public sources and must retrieve or escalate.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Engagement scope and professional signoff model
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Outsourced payroll engagements differ on whether the firm prepares, reviews, transmits, funds, files, or only packages payroll evidence. The pack treats engagement scope as required tenant context.

notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for a validated spec pack because all variable legal, jurisdictional, payment, and engagement facts are converted into required tenant facts and escalation triggers.
Research was constrained to authoritative public sources that cover workflow, evidence, terminology, confidentiality, professional boundaries, and failure handling.
